name: Illinois Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Illinois — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.

address.description: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation, 320 W. Washington Street, 3rd Floor, Springfield, IL 62786

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

requiredDocuments.name: Radiography Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

mainEntity.name: Does Illinois require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, Illinois does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in Illinois?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Illinois.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in Illinois?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Illinois?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Illinois. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.
